---
title: "News & Events"
description: "Seminars, awards, grants, and lab announcements."
---

## 2026

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.news-card}
**Seminar** · April 2026

### Public Seminar: Inequality Trends in Post-Pandemic Indonesia

Join us for a public seminar presenting new findings on income and consumption inequality
dynamics in Indonesia since 2020. The seminar will be held at the Faculty of Economics
and Business, Universitas Indonesia, and streamed online.

- **Speaker:** Krisna Gupta & Budi Santoso
- **Venue:** Room 201, FEB UI, Depok
- **Time:** 14:00–16:00 WIB
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.news-card}
**Award** · March 2026

### Best Paper Award at ASEAN Economic Congress

Congratulations to Dinda Rachmania and Yudo Prabowo, whose paper
*"Carbon Pricing in a Developing Economy: Labour Market and Distributional Effects"*
received the Best Paper award at the 2026 ASEAN Economic Congress held in Singapore.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.news-card}
**Grant** · February 2026

### New Research Grant from the World Bank

DEN Economics Lab has been awarded a World Bank research grant to study the impacts
of digital financial inclusion in rural Indonesia. The project will run for three years
and include randomised controlled trials in four provinces.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.news-card}
**Publication** · January 2026

### New Working Paper on Minimum Wages and Informality

Dinda Rachmania and Yudo Prabowo have released a new working paper using bunching
estimation to examine how minimum-wage hikes affect informal employment in Indonesia.
:::
:::

:::

---

## 2025

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.news-card}
**Workshop** · November 2025

### Annual Lab Retreat & Methods Workshop

DEN Economics Lab held its third annual research retreat at Puncak, West Java.
The two-day event featured presentations of ongoing projects and a methods workshop
on synthetic control methods led by Arya Pratama.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.news-card}
**Publication** · September 2025

### New Working Paper: Commodity Boom and Dutch Disease

Arya Pratama and Siti Aminah's working paper on the Indonesian commodity boom and
Dutch disease effects on manufacturing has been posted to SSRN and is under review
at the *Journal of Development Economics*.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.news-card}
**Media** · July 2025

### Krisna Gupta in Kompas: "Rethinking Trade Policy"

Lab director Krisna Gupta was featured in Kompas newspaper discussing the implications
of recent ASEAN trade agreements for Indonesian manufacturing and labour markets.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.news-card}
**Conference** · June 2025

### Presentations at ASSA Annual Meeting

Three papers from DEN Economics Lab were presented at the ASSA Annual Meeting in
San Francisco, covering trade, fiscal policy, and environmental economics.
:::
:::

:::

---

## Upcoming Events

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### 🗓️ May 2026 – Seminar Series
Monthly internal seminar: Rizky Aditya presents on poverty measurement and causal inference.
Open to all FEB UI students and faculty.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### 🎓 June 2026 – Summer School
One-week intensive course on Applied Econometrics for Master's and PhD students.
Applications open from April 2026. Seats are limited.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### 🌏 September 2026 – International Conference
DEN Economics Lab co-organises the 4th Indonesia Development Economics Conference
(IDEC 2026). Call for papers coming soon.
:::

:::
